_meta.description: Dated US federal tax tables consumed by the tax engine (design doc §8). Data, not code — update this file for a new tax year rather than touching engine logic.
_meta.verificationStatus: 2025 and 2026 ordinary/LTCG brackets, standard deductions, and SS bend points verified 2026-07-21 via IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-32 (2026) and cross-checked against multiple independent secondary sources (Tax Foundation, Kiplinger, Bipartisan Policy Center) — see per-year 'sources'. Reconciles the older pre-OBBBA figures previously in ../../Taxes/CLAUDE.md (2025 MFJ $30,000 / 2026 ~$30,600): those predate the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA, signed 2025-07-04), which raised the standard deduction and made the TCJA bracket structure permanent — the numbers below are current law. NOT yet modeled: OBBBA's separate temporary $6,000/qualifying-taxpayer 'senior deduction' (2025-2028, phases out above $75k single/$150k MFJ MAGI) — a known gap, layered on top of the standard age-65 addition below, not a replacement for it.
_meta.bracketFormat: Each bracket is {upTo, rate}. 'upTo' is the top of taxable income taxed at 'rate'; the final bracket uses upTo: null (no ceiling). Marginal, not effective.
sources: IRS Rev. Proc. (annual inflation adjustments) — ordinary + LTCG brackets, standard deduction
SSA — PIA bend points, National Average Wage Index (AWI), benefit-taxation thresholds
IRS Uniform Lifetime Table (Notice 2022-6 / Treas. Reg. 1.401(a)(9)-9) — RMD divisors

socialSecurityPIA._note: PIA = 0.90*min(AIME, b1) + 0.32*(portion between b1 and b2) + 0.15*(portion above b2). Bend points are wage-indexed per year.

fixed._note: Items NOT indexed to inflation — same nominal figures every year until Congress changes them.

socialSecurityBenefitTaxation._note: Provisional-income thresholds fixed by statute since 1983/1993 (design doc §6/§8) — never inflation-indexed, unlike almost everything else here. Up to 50% taxable above the first threshold, up to 85% above the second.

averageWageIndex._note: SSA National Average Wage Index series, used to index a person's past earnings before AIME (design doc §6). Populate from ssa.gov (AWI table). Indexing freezes at age 60; later years count at face value. Left empty — filled in Phase 5.

earlyClaimingReduction._note: Actuarial reduction for claiming before Full Retirement Age: ~5/9 of 1% per month for the first 36 months early, ~5/12 of 1% per month beyond that. Encode precisely in Phase 5.

rmd._note: SECURE 2.0's required-beginning-age step-up is BIRTH-YEAR based, not calendar-year based — a mistake in this file until 2026-07-21 (it read 'age 73, rising to 75 in 2033', which is a common but incorrect simplification). Correct rule: age 73 for those born 1951-1959, age 75 for those born 1960 or later. See requiredBeginningAgeByBirthYear.
rmd._uniformLifetimeNote: Ages 72-100 cross-checked against multiple published sources (verified 2026-07-21). Ages 101-119 are UNVERIFIED approximations (could not reach an authoritative source through this environment's network access) — the engine clamps/logs rather than silently trusting them; re-verify against IRS Pub. 590-B Table III before relying on projections into these ages. Age 120+ divisor of 2.0 IS confirmed (published floor value).
